author: anon-a7c6025f
content:
Plan: Current PTX kernels load/store one f32 per thread (32-bit memory transactions). GPU memory bandwidth is optimized for 128-bit (16-byte) transactions. Using ld.global.v4.f32 loads 4 floats per instruction, quadrupling memory throughput for bandwidth-bound kernels.

Approach: Create vectorized variants of the most-used kernels (add, mul, sub, neg, relu, scale). Each thread processes 4 elements. Tail elements handled by a scalar epilogue. Wire the vectorized kernels into the try_launch_unary/binary paths.
